old_claim.note: Lithium-Ion Batteries (Rechargeable Power) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because EV charging infrastructure is chemistry-agnostic and can serve rechargeable electric vehicles without being limited to lithium-ion cells.

source_shape.source_locator: DOE/NREL history of plug-in electric vehicle charging infrastructure and AFDC infrastructure tracking.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source frames charging infrastructure around electric vehicle supply equipment and public infrastructure, not a single battery chemistry.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Charging networks require power electronics and rechargeable EV batteries generally; a lithium-ion-only prerequisite confuses dominant modern vehicle chemistry with infrastructure fundamentals.

ontology_before: EV charging networks depended on lithium-ion cells, making the network category unnecessarily chemistry-specific.
ontology_after: EV charging networks now depend on the broader electrochemical battery lineage, smart-grid context, and power electronics.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-07-semiconductor-energy-agriculture-chronology.json keeps this removed edge absent and documents the corrected dependency scope.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to mean modern lithium-ion EV fast-charging networks only.
A better generic EV battery node were added and should replace portable_electrochemical_batteries.
A source showed charging networks as defined here cannot be built for non-lithium electric vehicles.
short_reason: EV charging infrastructure is not inherently lithium-ion-specific.
